flagged_messages.message_text: ROW-7461 (Received): 'You weren't present and involved in anything until I recently pointed out all the things you don't do that would easily give me full custody' | ROW-7481 (Received): 'I don't have to do as much as I do to include you' | ROW-7482 (Received): 'I don't have to give you pictures of her'
flagged_messages.primary_reasoning: Two RF-05 signal elements within COMP-RF04-018 (RF-04+RF-15+RF-01+RF-05 per RF-04 pass). SIGNAL 1 — ROW-7461: 'You weren't present and involved in anything until I recently pointed out all the things you don't do that would easily give me full custody' — implicit full-custody viability claim. Distinct from B-017's explicit filing threat (ROW-6312 'I'm going to file for sole custody') — this formulation claims: (a) Melissa has already identified the basis for a full custody case; (b) Eli's current behavior improvements are attributed to Melissa's threat rather than his own judgment; (c) the threat remains operative as the compliance mechanism. ADD-002: Sev-1 (not first instance of custody threat category; B-017 established that). The implicit framing ('would easily give me full custody') rather than explicit ('I am filing') = Sev-1. SIGNAL 2 — ROW-7481-7482 access withdrawal threats: 'I don't have to do as much as I do to include you' + 'I don't have to give you pictures of her' — access-conditional framing: Melissa positions Eli's current access level as her personal gift, not his legal right. These are more RF-02 primary (gatekeeping leverage) but function as RF-05 threats in context — threatening withdrawal of access/information as compliance instrument. ADD-002: Sev-1 (adjacent to contempt threat category; not first instance of access-conditional framing). Combined Sev-1 for the cluster.
flagged_messages.context_notes: All three rows are part of the Oct 3 mega-cluster (ROW-7325-7490), the largest single conflict episode in B-019. COMP-RF04-018 (RF-04+RF-15+RF-01+RF-05) is confirmed per RF-04 pass. RF-04 peripheral flag RF04-B019-P05 explicitly cross-tags both ROW-7461 and ROW-7481-7482 as RF-05 primary. The 8th predation framing (ROW-7367 'Did you tell the Dr you made her a new sister/cousin') within this same cluster is ADD-001 exempt — RF-04+RF-15 primary, not RF-05. The gatekeeping control admission (ROW-7478-7483 'I used to ask her if she wanted to call you in the mornings') is RF-02 primary.
flagged_messages.quote_excerpt: 'You weren't present and involved in anything until I recently pointed out all the things you don't do that would easily give me full custody' (ROW-7461); 'I don't have to do as much as I do to include you' (ROW-7481)

arc_state_snapshot.note: ARC-RF05-001 ACTIVE. B-019 adds S-018: implicit custody viability claim + access withdrawal threats within COMP-RF04-018. Sev-1 (prior instance of custody threat category established in B-017). The custody threat pattern is confirmed operative in two consecutive batches (B-017 Aug 4, B-019 Oct 3).

batch_observations.tone_assessment: B-019 (Sep-Oct 2024): Moderate-signal RF-05 batch. Oct 3 mega-cluster contains implicit custody threat embedded in a comprehensive RF-04+RF-15+RF-01 compound. The implicit custody framing (ROW-7461) is significant because it reveals Melissa's awareness of the legal basis for her custody threat and her self-attribution as the force that has driven Eli's behavioral compliance. Sep 15 physical harm cluster (COMP-RF04-017: choking claim, DV false allegation) is RF-04+RF-15+RF-11 — no RF-05 primary.
batch_observations.cross_rf_context: RF-04 B-019: COMP-RF04-017 (Sep 15 choking claim RF-04+RF-15+RF-11) + COMP-RF04-018 (Oct 3 mega-cluster RF-04+RF-15+RF-01+RF-05). RF-05 confirmed component of COMP-RF04-018. Oct 18 Abby-causal-attribution (ROW-7553) is RF-04 primary Sev-1.
